steps.title: Identify which gene drives A. sicerae survival
steps.content: Type A flies (with DptA silenced) maintain ~95% survival throughout A. sicerae infection, while Type B flies (with DptB silenced) plummet to nearly 0% by day 10. This survival divergence proves that DptB, not DptA, is essential for A. sicerae defense.

steps.title: Match DptB's role to the answer choice
steps.content:
Choice B asserts that DptB may have evolved as a specific defense against A. sicerae.

The survival curves confirm it: keeping DptB intact (Type A) preserves resistance, while silencing DptB (Type B) destroys it.

Choices A, C, and D all claim DptA or both genes matter for A. sicerae resistance, but Type A's robust survival with DptA off eliminates all three.

DptB's isolation as the sole driver of A. sicerae resistance directly validates B's hypothesis of specialized defense.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
